title: Cream of Mushroom Soup
tagline: Velvety soup of deeply browned mushrooms in a garlicky thyme cream.
servingsNote: About 250 g mushrooms makes a rich bowl for 2 as a starter, or a light meal.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients. Rich but protein-poor — mushrooms are mostly water and the calories are butter + cream.
Lean it out: use whole milk thickened with the flour roux and just a small splash of cream at the end instead of a full 100 ml. Cuts ~120-150 kcal per serving.

Protein boost (soya doesn't suit a smooth soup): stir 2-3 tbsp Greek yogurt into each bowl off the heat, or add ~120 g shredded cooked chicken. A scoop of unflavoured whey whisked in off-heat also works if you want a pure macro hit.

items: 250 g mushrooms (button/cremini), sliced (save a few for garnish)
400 ml vegetable stock (or water + 1 stock cube)
A few reserved sauteed mushroom slices
1 tbsp chopped parsley or extra thyme

steps.title: Brown the mushrooms hard
steps.body: Melt 2 tbsp (30 g) of the butter in a pot on medium-high. Add the 250 g sliced mushrooms and cook 8-10 minutes. They'll release a lot of water first — keep going until ALL of it evaporates and the mushrooms are properly browned. This browning is the entire flavour of the soup, so don't rush it. Scoop out a few slices for garnish.

steps.title: Soften the aromatics
steps.body: Add the last 1 tbsp (15 g) butter, then the chopped onion (~80 g) and 2 minced garlic cloves. Cook 3-4 minutes until soft, not coloured.

steps.title: Make the roux
steps.body: Sprinkle in 2 tbsp (16 g) flour and stir constantly for 1-2 minutes to cook off the raw taste — this is what thickens the soup to that velvety body.

steps.title: Add liquid and simmer
steps.body: Slowly pour in 400 ml stock while stirring so no lumps form, then add 150 ml milk, 1/2 tsp thyme and 1/2 tsp black pepper. Simmer gently 8-10 minutes until it thickens slightly.

steps.title: Blend (optional)
steps.body: For a smooth soup, blend about two-thirds of it (immersion blender or jug) and leave the rest chunky for texture. Skip this step entirely if you like it rustic and chunky.

steps.title: Finish with cream
steps.body: Stir in 80-100 ml cream, warm through on low (don't boil hard or it can split), and season with salt to taste. Serve topped with the reserved mushrooms, a swirl of cream and chopped parsley or thyme.

notes: Drying the mushrooms out completely before anything else goes in is the make-or-break step — watery mushrooms = flat, thin soup.
No cream? Extra milk + the roux still gives a creamy body; a spoon of Greek yogurt off the heat adds tang and protein.
Scales cleanly — double everything and use ~500 g mushrooms for 4.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
